commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b6eb14acd8e11ea79697b5e39930c1c73e3aa09c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes cost usage daily bucket selection so Today-labeled menu/dashboard/API usage surfaces use the current local day instead of the latest historical bucket, with tests and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible: `CostUsageFetcher.tokenSnapshot` selects the newest parsed historical daily row and assigns it to session fields that feed Today-labeled UI.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor proof plus narrow code/test fixes before merge; automation should not mark this repaired while real behavior proof is still missing.

target.title: Fix: "Today" cost shows current local day, not the latest historical bucket (#1705)
target.body:
Fixes #1705.

## Problem

UI surfaces labeled **Today** showed the newest non-empty *historical* cost bucket instead of the actual current local day. With a gap after the latest recorded usage day, the menu card, inline dashboard, and OpenAI/Claude Admin + Poe summaries kept reporting an old day's usage as "today".

## Fix

Invariant: **a "Today" label is always the current local day â€” `$0.00 Â· 0 tokens` when there is no usage today â€” never a latest-bucket helper.**

- **`CostUsageTokenSnapshot`**: add `localDayKey`, `entry(in:forLocalDayContaining:)`, `latestEntry(in:)`, `currentDayEntry()`.
- **`tokenSnapshot`**: session (Today) fields come from the current local day â€” today's row â†’ its values; history but no today row â†’ **zero**; no history â†’ nil. A `sessionDay` mode keeps **Bedrock** on *Latest billing day* semantics (AWS Cost Explorer lags), and Mistral keeps its existing latest path.
- Every Today-labeled consumer updated to the current local day: `MenuDescriptor` (OpenAI/Claude Admin, Poe), `InlineUsageDashboardContent` (usage notes + dashboards + cost-history KPI), OpenAI `toCostUsageTokenSnapshot`. New `currentDay()` accessors on the OpenAI/Claude/Poe snapshots (zero when no bucket for today).
- The historical chart rows and "Last 30 days" totals are untouched.

## Tests

New `CostUsageTodayBucketTests` (drives `tokenSnapshot`: today-zero, today-populated, emptyâ†’nil, `latestEntry`, boundary); existing fixtures pinned to deterministic days. `swift build` + `swift test` green (pre-existing timing-flaky suites aside).

## Known limitation (follow-up)

The OpenAI/Claude Admin and Poe provider APIs return usage **pre-aggregated by UTC day**. `currentDay()` resolves the local day from each bucket's absolute `startTime` (Poe compares its `yyyy-MM-dd` string), so the multi-day "stale bucket as Today" bug is fixed, but a perfectly local "Today" around UTC/local midnight boundaries would require those fetchers to pull hourly granularity and re-aggregate by `Calendar.current` â€” out of scope here. The local cost-usage path (the issue's subject) is fully local-correct.
target.author: leolin990405
